UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile and personal settings within the MemoryLane application. This entity stores metadata about the user, but not authentication credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This typically corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID.

email.description: The user's email address, used for display and potentially communication (not for authentication system data).

profileImageUrl.description: Optional URI to the user's profile image stored in Firebase Storage.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

Scrapbook.title: Scrapbook
Scrapbook.description: Represents a digital scrapbook, containing metadata and references to its pages and collaborators.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Scrapbook entity.

description.description: An optional brief description of the scrapbook's content or theme.

ownerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created and primarily owns this scrapbook. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Scrapbook)

category.description: The thematic category of the scrapbook (e.g., 'Holiday', 'Birthday', 'Travel', 'Family').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the scrapbook was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the scrapbook was last updated.

Page.title: Page
Page.description: Represents a single page within a scrapbook, containing an ordered array of multimedia canvas objects.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Page entity.

scrapbookId.description: Reference to the parent Scrapbook entity to which this page belongs. (Relationship: Scrapbook 1:N Page)

pageNumber.description: The sequential number or order of the page within its scrapbook, starting from 1.

canvasObjects.description: An ordered array of multimedia elements (images, videos, audio) and their display properties on this page's canvas.

CanvasObject.title: CanvasObject
CanvasObject.description: Represents a single multimedia element (image, video, audio) placed on a scrapbook page, along with its display properties on the creative canvas.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CanvasObject entity. This allows individual tracking and manipulation of canvas items.

pageId.description: Reference to the Page entity on which this canvas object is placed. (Relationship: Page 1:N CanvasObject)

mediaUri.description: The URI link to the actual media content (image, video, or audio) stored in Firebase Storage.

x.description: The X-coordinate position of the object's top-left corner on the canvas, relative to the page's coordinate system.

y.description: The Y-coordinate position of the object's top-left corner on the canvas, relative to the page's coordinate system.

rotation.description: The rotation angle of the object in degrees (e.g., 0-359).

scale.description: The uniform scale factor applied to the object (e.g., 1.0 for original size, 0.5 for half size).

zIndex.description: The layering order of the object on the canvas. Higher values appear in front of lower values.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the canvas object was added to the page.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the canvas object's properties were last updated.

ScrapbookPermission.title: ScrapbookPermission
ScrapbookPermission.description: Manages access control for a user on a specific scrapbook, defining their permission level (e.g., viewer, editor).

id.description: Unique identifier for the ScrapbookPermission entity.

scrapbookId.description: Reference to the Scrapbook entity for which this permission is granted. (Relationship: Scrapbook 1:N ScrapbookPermission)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity who is granted this permission. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ScrapbookPermission)

permissionLevel.description: The level of access the user has on the scrapbook (e.g., 'viewer', 'editor').

invitedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who invited this collaborator. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ScrapbookPermission)

invitedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the collaboration invitation was issued or accepted.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the authenticated user matching the {userId} wildcard. This path ensures path-based ownership.

Collection for all scrapbooks. Each document includes denormalized 'ownerId' and a 'members' map (e.g., {uid1: 'owner', uid2: 'editor'}) for authorization independence and collaborative access control. Rules will leverage the 'members' map for secure list and read operations.

params.name: scrapbookId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific scrapbook.

definition.description: Subcollection of pages belonging to a specific scrapbook. Each page document denormalizes 'ownerId' and the 'members' map from its parent scrapbook for authorization independence.

params.name: scrapbookId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent scrapbook.

params.name: pageId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific page within the scrapbook.

definition.description: Subcollection of multimedia canvas objects within a specific page. Each CanvasObject document denormalizes 'ownerId' and the 'members' map from the root scrapbook for authorization independence.

params.name: scrapbookId
params.description: The unique identifier of the grandparent scrapbook.

params.name: pageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent page.

params.name: canvasObjectId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific canvas object.

definition.description: Stores pending invitations for a user to collaborate on a scrapbook. This collection is user-specific, allowing a user to list their invitations. The 'permissionLevel' here reflects the *proposed* access. Active permissions are managed via the denormalized 'members' map on the Scrapbook document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who received the invitation.

params.name: invitationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the invitation, typically referencing the scrapbookId.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for the MemoryLane app is designed to address the `PERMISSION_DENIED` error on `/scrapbooks` by strictly adhering to Authorization Independence and QAP principles, specifically for collaborative data. The core strategy involves denormalizing authorization contexts directly into each relevant document.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization (Addressing the Error):**

The initial error "Missing or insufficient permissions: ... list ... /databases/(default)/documents/scrapbooks" indicates that the security rules for the top-level `/scrapbooks` collection were too broad or lacked the necessary per-document authorization checks, preventing users from listing only their accessible scrapbooks. To fix this and ensure rules are simple and don't rely on `get()` calls, the following denormalization strategy is implemented:

1.  **Scrapbook Documents (`/scrapbooks/{scrapbookId}`):** Each `Scrapbook` document will include an `ownerId` field (the creator's UID) and, critically, a `members` map (`members: { [uid: string]: 'owner' | 'editor' | 'viewer' }`). This map lists all users who have access to the scrapbook and their respective roles. This denormalization allows security rules to check `request.auth.uid in resource.data.members` directly on the `Scrapbook` document without needing to perform a `get()` operation on a separate permissions collection or a parent document.

2.  **Page Documents (`/scrapbooks/{scrapbookId}/pages/{pageId}`):** Each `Page` document will also denormalize the `ownerId` and the `members` map from its parent `Scrapbook`. This ensures that access to a page can be determined independently by examining the page document itself, enabling atomic creation/updates and simplifying rules (e.g., `allow write: if resource.data.members[request.auth.uid] == 'editor'`).

3.  **CanvasObject Documents (`/scrapbooks/{scrapbookId}/pages/{pageId}/canvasObjects/{canvasObjectId}`):** Similarly, each `CanvasObject` document will include the `ownerId` and the `members` map, ensuring granular and independent access control at the deepest level of the hierarchy.

**QAPs (Query as Authorization Principles) and `list` operations:**

*   **For `/scrapbooks`:** To enable secure `list` operations without security rules acting as filters, client-side queries for `/scrapbooks` **must** include a `where` clause that filters documents based on the `members` map, for example: `db.collection('scrapbooks').where(new FieldPath('members', currentUser.uid), '!=', null)`. The Firestore security rule for `list` on `/scrapbooks` can then simply check `allow list: if request.auth.uid != null;` because the `read` rule for individual `Scrapbook` documents will enforce `resource.data.members[request.auth.uid] != null` for every document retrieved by the query. If a client attempts to list *all* scrapbooks without filtering, or queries for scrapbooks they are not a member of, the `list` operation will fail when the `read` rule is applied to the individual documents in the result set.
*   **For `/users/{userId}/scrapbookInvitations`:** QAPs are naturally satisfied here as access is path-based. A user can only list invitations explicitly addressed to their `uid` (`/users/{request.auth.uid}/scrapbookInvitations`).

**Structural Segregation and Access Modeling:**

*   **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`):** User profiles are stored in a top-level collection `users`, using path-based ownership (`/users/{request.auth.uid}`). This ensures private data is easily secured to the owner.
*   **Scrapbooks and Subcollections (`/scrapbooks/{scrapbookId}/...`):** Collaborative scrapbooks are kept in a top-level `scrapbooks` collection. Pages and CanvasObjects are modeled as subcollections to reflect their hierarchical relationship, with denormalized authorization (as explained above) to maintain Authorization Independence.
*   **Scrapbook Invitations (`/users/{userId}/scrapbookInvitations/{invitationId}`):** Instead of storing active permissions, the `ScrapbookPermission` entity is repurposed for `ScrapbookInvitations`. These are stored under the invited user's path, allowing a user to easily list and manage their own pending invitations. Once an invitation is accepted, the `members` map on the `Scrapbook` (and its subcollections) is updated, and the invitation document can be removed or marked as accepted. This maintains a clear separation of concerns between pending invitations and active permissions.
